summary: Baseline: yield=0.786, coherence=0.670, wave4_floor=0.429
No novelty cliff with full peer review — model overcorrects at frontier domains
No specialization floor at nominal novelty — generalist baseline too high, peer review absorbs gap
Wave ceiling at wave 7 (final_wave_yield=0.143 < floor 0.15); plateau at 29 actionable beyond wave 8
Optimum: 12 q/wave (29 actionable); default 7 q/wave efficient at 78.6%; WARNING at 15+
Empirically 0% OVERRIDE in 14 peer-reviewed findings; BASE_SPECIALIST/GENERALIST_ACCURACY are unvalidated assumptions
Signal rate 4.4x increase from early to mid waves (15% to 65%) — adaptive expansion confirmed, simulation saturation model is backwards
Peer review applied in only waves 13-16 (4 of 36 waves), stopped after wave 16; format inconsistency detected
Fix loop divergence confirmed — double-decay bug re-checked 18 consecutive waves; reconcile audit 5 consecutive; simulation cap of 2 attempts not enforced
Empirical novelty curve is J-shaped (low early, high late) — opposite of simulation's monotonic decay; simulation model is structurally inverted
Only 9 failure themes; retrieval+decay dominate 83% of findings; 6+ likely failure categories have near-zero coverage; hypothesis generator clustering detected
Synthesis coherence excellent — correctly tracks 7 FALSE NEGATIVE reversals, 6-finding investigation chains, 4 deployment blockers; estimated coherence 0.87
81.9% novel questions; 8.5% deployment re-checks (true redundancy); 9.6% productive follow-ups; simulation 15% saturation slightly overestimates real rate
Fix convergence rate ~8% — 23+ open failures at wave 36; human deployment bottleneck is not modeled; BrickLayer is a diagnosis loop not a fix loop
0 fix-introduced regressions in 2 deployed fixes; simulation 8% regression rate is too pessimistic for carefully targeted fixes in tested codebase
15.4% INCONCLUSIVE rate from external timing dependencies (cron windows, GC gates); simulation timeout model doesn't capture multi-wave blocking chains
BrickLayer already portable to any codebase; 4-step setup (~5-6h); Recall campaign proves multi-service stack portability
Research/business portability inherent — evidence collection replaces simulation; bricklayer-meta itself is this use case
Creative domain partially portable — works for constraint-based auditing; fails for aesthetic quality; SUBJECTIVE verdict needed
85% universal already; 4 changes (SUBJECTIVE verdict, runner contract, evaluate.py interface, diagnosis/deployment split) complete the framework; ~9 days
Four recalibrations interact destructively at 4-wave baseline; compromise model works: cliff at 0.75, floor at spec=0.15
DEPLOYMENT_BLOCKED mechanism: 3-consecutive-FAILURE trigger, git diff detection, 5th status value, ~12-14 slots freed in Recall campaign
J-curve piecewise model achieves RMSE=0.077 (6.6x better than decay); Model B validated; recommended replacement for _wave_uniqueness()
HHI sentinel fires wave 1 (write-path monopoly), not wave 8-16; retrieval+decay cluster invisible at wave granularity; coverage gain ~7 (below 10 threshold)
Root cause B (session context): peer review absent in Session A which pre-dated program.md peer-review instruction; two sessions ran campaign; no auto-disable code exists
